240	\paragraph{The gap and route~(e), superseded.} The substrate
241	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\) of this chapter is the
242	sheafification of \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\), and
243	sheafification of presheaves of modules is exactly the localization
244	\(L = \mathtt{sheafification}\) at the locally-bijective class \(W = J.W\). Route~(e)
245	proposed to obtain a \emph{full} monoidal structure on
246	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\)
247	by applying \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) (ii) to the already-monoidal category (i),
248	which would require the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (its two
249	whiskering-stability fields \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}, with load-bearing
250	local-injectivity half \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}). This is
251	\emph{not} how the group law is realized. The group law consumes only the
252	\emph{existence} of the three coherence isomorphisms, and the associator is built
253	directly by gluing canonical local isomorphisms through
254	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}); it never invokes
255	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), a coherent monoidal category, or the stalkwise
256	whiskering apparatus. The route~(e) instance is therefore vestigial and off the
257	critical path.
258	
259	The stalk-infrastructure analysis that route~(e) turned on is recorded here for
260	reference only, since that route is superseded. The \emph{stalk module structure}
261	is already present in Mathlib: the file
262	\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk} supplies, for a topological
263	space \(X\), a presheaf of commutative rings \(R\) on \(X\) and a point
264	\(x \in X\), the \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-module structure on the stalk
265	\(\mathtt{stalk}\,M.\mathtt{presheaf}\,x\) of a presheaf of modules \(M\),
266	together with the germ--scalar compatibility \(\mathtt{germ\_smul}\); a linearity
267	packaging of the induced stalk map of a morphism was built project-side
268	(\cref{lem:stalk_linear_map}, the \emph{linearity half} of ingredient (d.1)).
269	Route~(e) additionally required a (d.1)-\emph{bridge} between the site-level
270	\(J.W\) and the topological stalkwise-isomorphism criterion on
271	\(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\), and (d.2) the commutation \((A \otimes^{p} B)_x \cong A_x
272	\otimes_{R_x} B_x\) of the stalk with the relative module-presheaf tensor; there is
273	moreover no monoidal \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\) in Mathlib. Because the group law
274	is realized through the direct-gluing associator (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso},
275	via \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}) and never through
276	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), none of this stalk apparatus is on the critical path;
277	it is retained only to document the superseded route.
278	
279	The project's policy on this gap is \emph{project-side}: the substrate is
280	built inside \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/} (the file
281	\(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean}\) is its home)
282	and the consumer instance is closed against it. A parallel Mathlib upstream
283	PR with the same content is welcome but not on the critical path for the
284	project; the consumer is closed against the project-side substrate
285	independently of any upstream timing.
286	
287	\section{The substrate \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)}
288	\label{sec:tensorobj_substrate}
289	
290	\begin{definition}
291	
292	\leanok
293	[The substrate operation $\otimes$ on $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$]
294	  \label{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
295	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
296	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. For
297	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), define
298	  \[
299	    M \otimes_X N \;\;\in\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
300	  \]
301	  as follows. Choose an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}_{i \in I}\)
302	  of \(X\). On each \(U_i\), the restriction
303	  \(M|_{U_i}\) corresponds (via the equivalence between
304	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A_i}\)-modules and \(A_i\)-modules) to an
305	  \(A_i\)-module \(M_i\), and similarly
306	  \(N|_{U_i}\) corresponds to an \(A_i\)-module \(N_i\). Define
307	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\) as the sheafification of the presheaf
308	  \(U_i \mapsto M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i\). On overlaps
309	  \(U_i \cap U_j = \Spec A_{ij}\) the resulting modules agree via the
310	  canonical compatibility
311	  \((M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i) \otimes_{A_i} A_{ij}
312	     \;\cong\;
313	     (M_j \otimes_{A_j} N_j) \otimes_{A_j} A_{ij}\),
314	  so the data \(\{(M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\}\) glue to a single object
315	  \(M \otimes_X N\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The result is
316	  canonically independent of the affine cover chosen.
317	
318	  Equivalently: the underlying presheaf of \(M \otimes_X N\) is the
319	  presheaf-of-modules tensor product
320	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) of the underlying
321	  presheaves of \(M\) and \(N\), composed with the sheafification
322	  functor on the small Zariski site of \(X\). The substrate operation
323	  \(\otimes_X\) is, by construction, a \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
324	  object reflecting the relative tensor product of
325	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described in Stacks tag 03DM.
326	\end{definition}
327	
328	\begin{lemma}
329	
330	\leanok
331	[Functoriality of $\otimes_X$]
332	  \label{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
333	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality}
334	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
335	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The assignment
336	  \((M, N) \mapsto M \otimes_X N\) of
337	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} extends to a bifunctor
338	  \[
339	    \otimes_X \;\;\colon\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\times\;
340	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\;\longrightarrow\;\;
341	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
342	  \]
343	  natural in both arguments: a pair of morphisms
344	  \(f : M \to M'\) and \(g : N \to N'\) determines a morphism
345	  \(f \otimes g : M \otimes_X N \to M' \otimes_X N'\) compatible with
346	  identities and composition. This bifunctorial action on morphisms is
347	  the only datum the present lemma provides. The further coherence data
348	  of a monoidal structure --- the unitors
349	  \[
350	    \lambda_M \;\colon\; \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X M \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
351	    \qquad
352	    \rho_M \;\colon\; M \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
353	  \]
354	  the associator
355	  \(\alpha_{M, N, P} : (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim}
356	   M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\), and the braiding
357	  \(\beta_{M, N} : M \otimes_X N \xrightarrow{\sim} N \otimes_X M\) ---
358	  are \emph{not} outputs of this lemma; they are off the critical path,
359	  recorded in \cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
360	\end{lemma}
361	
362	\begin{proof}
363	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
364	  Pick an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}\) of \(X\). On each
365	  affine, the assignment is the standard tensor product of modules over a
366	  commutative ring \(A_i\), which is bifunctorial. The bifunctorial action
367	  on morphisms glues across the cover because the underlying gluing data of
368	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} respect the canonical morphisms on
369	  overlaps; equivalently, the morphism action is inherited from
370	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) (Mathlib module
371	  \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}) under
372	  sheafification. The unitor/associator/braiding coherence data are
373	  \emph{not} constructed here; they are off the critical path
374	  (\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}).
375	\end{proof}
376	
377	\begin{remark}
378	[The full monoidal category on all of $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$ is off-path; the group law consumes only propositions]
379	  \label{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}
380	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
381	        lem:whisker_of_W}
382	  One could ask for the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) of
383	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, together with
384	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\), \(\alpha\), \(\lambda\), \(\rho\) and \(\beta\), to
385	  assemble into a full symmetric \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
386	  \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), discharging the pentagon,
387	  triangle and hexagon coherence identities. This is explicitly \emph{off-path}
388	  and \emph{not pursued}. One could in principle obtain it as the output of
389	  \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) applied to the
390	  already-monoidal presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)
391	  and the sheafification localizer \(J.W\), supplying the instance
392	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}); but the group law never
393	  consumes a coherent monoidal category, so this construction is superseded and not
394	  carried out. The substrate provides only the \emph{existence} of the three
395	  coherence isomorphisms that the group law actually consumes.
396	
397	  Were that superseded route pursued, the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\)
398	  would need \emph{no} closed structure: its
399	  \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) field \(W\,g \Rightarrow W(F \triangleleft g)\) for an
400	  \emph{arbitrary} module \(F\) is the flatness-free stalkwise statement
401	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} (a \(J.W\)-morphism \(g\) is a
402	  stalkwise isomorphism, so \((F \triangleleft g)_x = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes
403	  g_x\) is again an isomorphism for any \(F\)), the same technique Mathlib uses for
404	  fixed-base presheaves in
405	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\). It would use neither
406	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) nor the
407	  sectionwise-flatness hypothesis of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} (which is
408	  false over a non-affine open and is likewise off the critical path). But this
409	  apparatus is not on the critical path at all: the group law is realized without
410	  it.
411	
412	  Independently of how the monoidal structure is built, the relative Picard
413	  group law of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is a structure on
414	  \emph{isomorphism classes} of line bundles, and every group axiom there is a
415	  \emph{proposition} of the form \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\).
416	  Such a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it, so the
417	  coherence data of the monoidal category --- pentagon, triangle, hexagon, and
418	  a fortiori any monoidal-closed structure --- is never \emph{consumed} by the
419	  group law. The group law needs only the existence of the three coherence
420	  isomorphisms \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,%
421	  lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, supplied directly: the associator by gluing canonical
422	  local isomorphisms through the restriction-compatibility isomorphism
423	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}), and the
424	  unitors and braiding by the cheap \(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern
425	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}). The carrier --- iso-classes
426	  of the substrate tensor \(\otimes_X\) --- is the scheme-theoretic analogue of
427	  Mathlib's Picard group \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R := \mathrm{Skeleton}(
428	  \mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}), but it
429	  is built \emph{differently}: Mathlib reads its \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) off the
430	  \(\mathrm{Skeleton}\) of the coherent monoidal category
431	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalCategory}(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) over a fixed ring,
432	  whereas here --- with no coherent monoidal category on
433	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) available for the varying structure sheaf --- the
434	  group is assembled by hand from the existence-of-isomorphism facts.
435	\end{remark}
436	
437	\section{The lift through \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)}
438	\label{sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
439	
440	The consumer in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} works not with
441	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\) directly but with the subtype
442	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) of locally-trivial
443	modules on the fiber product \(C \times_k T\), introduced in
444	\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}. This section records the
445	\emph{existence-of-isomorphism facts on line bundles} (associativity, unit,
446	commutativity and inverse) that the relative Picard group law consumes. These facts
447	are supplied \emph{directly}: associativity by the direct-gluing associator
448	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (through the linchpin
449	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}), unit and commutativity by the cheap
450	\(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}),
451	and the inverse existentially from \(\otimes\)-invertibility. They are \emph{not}
452	read off any \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure: the route that would derive them from
453	\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal} (route~(e)) is superseded and off-path. The section also
454	records the closure of \(\otimes\) on the locally-trivial
455	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\) subtype (\cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}) and
456	the interaction with the pullback functor \(\pi_T^*\). The open-immersion
457	restriction-compatibility isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is on the
458	critical path precisely because it is consumed by the direct-gluing associator
459	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}; it is \emph{not} tied to the superseded
460	whiskering-stability lemma \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, which belongs
461	to the off-path route~(e).
462	
463	\begin{lemma}
464	
465	\leanok
466	[Sectionwise lax-monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\)]
467	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal}
468	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal}
469	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
470	  % NOTE: this CommRingCat-level lax-monoidal instance
471	  % (`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`, with helpers
472	  % `restrictScalarsLaxε`, `restrictScalarsLaxμ`) is a self-contained
473	  % supplement. It is NOT used by `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, whose proof
474	  % (open-immersion sectionwise base change) identifies restriction along an
475	  % open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
476	  % needs no lax-monoidal pushforward. Retained for potential reuse; off the
477	  % critical path for the tensor group law.
478	  Let \(\varphi : R \to S\) be a morphism of presheaves of \emph{commutative}
479	  rings on a site (so that both \(R\) and \(S\) factor through
480	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) and the category of presheaves of modules over each
481	  is monoidal). Then the presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor
482	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) is lax monoidal.
483	  Consequently, composing with the already-monoidal Mathlib functor
484	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) via
485	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\), the full pushforward
486	  \[
487	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi
488	      \;\;:=\;\;
489	    \mathtt{pushforward}_0\,F\,R \;\circ\; \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi
490	  \]
491	  is lax monoidal. This lemma is a self-contained
492	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-level lax-monoidal supplement: it stands on its own
493	  and is retained for potential reuse, but it is \emph{off the critical path}
494	  for the tensor group law. In particular it is \emph{not} an ingredient of
495	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, whose proof identifies restriction along an
496	  open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
497	  consequently needs no lax-monoidal structure on the pushforward.
498	\end{lemma}
499	
500	\begin{proof}
501	
502	  This is a \emph{sectionwise} lift of the existing fact that, for a ring map
503	  \(f : R \to S\), the module restriction-of-scalars functor
504	  \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,f\) is lax monoidal
505	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction}, where it is
506	  obtained as the \(\mathtt{rightAdjointLaxMonoidal}\) of the
507	  extension/restriction adjunction \(\mathtt{extendRestrictScalarsAdj}\) ---
508	  i.e.\ Mathlib already uses the same mate idiom one level down). The presheaf
509	  monoidal structure is computed sectionwise,
510	  \((M_1 \otimes M_2).\mathrm{obj}\,X = M_1.\mathrm{obj}\,X \otimes M_2.\mathrm{obj}\,X\),
511	  and the presheaf functor \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) acts
512	  sectionwise as \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{app}\,X)\).
513	  Hence the lax structure maps \(\mu\) (laxity) and \(\varepsilon\) (unit)
514	  assemble from the per-section \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) ones, the required
515	  naturality squares being the naturality of the sectionwise maps against the
516	  presheaf restriction morphisms. Packaging these as a
517	  \(\mathtt{Functor.CoreLaxMonoidal}\) yields the lax-monoidal structure; no
518	  new diagram chase is needed beyond transporting the sectionwise coherence.
519	  The composite \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) is then lax monoidal by
520	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\) together with the monoidality of
521	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) (Mathlib), modulo a defeq
522	  transport across the definitional unfolding of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\).
523	
524	  The commutativity hypothesis is essential: the presheaf-of-modules monoidal
525	  category requires \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-valued presheaves of rings, so the
526	  instance must be stated over \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-factored \(R, S\). In
527	  the project this holds: the relevant \(\varphi\) is
528	  \((\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\), which is
529	  structure-sheaf-valued and hence commutative-ring-valued.
530	\end{proof}
531	
532	\begin{lemma}
533	
534	\leanok
535	[Tensor product commutes with restriction along an open immersion]
536	  \label{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
537	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_restrict_iso}
538	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
539	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, let \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be
540	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, and let
541	  \(f : U \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion, with \((-)|_f\) the
542	  restriction of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along \(f\). The canonical comparison
543	  morphism
544	  \[
545	    (M \otimes_X N)\big|_f
546	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\;}\;
547	    M\big|_f \;\otimes_U\; N\big|_f
548	  \]
549	  is an isomorphism. No local-freeness, flatness, or line-bundle hypothesis on
550	  \(M, N\) is required: the statement holds for all \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
551	  The reason is structural to open immersions --- restriction along \(f\) is
552	  base change along the structure-sheaf \emph{isomorphism} induced by \(f\),
553	  not along a general ring map --- and base change along a ring isomorphism is
554	  trivially invertible and commutes with tensor products.
555	\end{lemma}
556	
557	\begin{proof}
558	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
559	  Write \(\varphi\) for the morphism of structure-sheaf-valued presheaves of
560	  rings induced by the open immersion \(f\) (concretely
561	  \(\varphi = (\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\)), so that
562	  restriction along \(f\) is computed by the inverse-image (pullback) functor on
563	  presheaves of modules. The proof exploits the defining feature of an open
564	  immersion: the comparison of structure-sheaf rings over the image is an
565	  \emph{isomorphism}, not merely a ring map. For an open \(V \subseteq U\) the
566	  section ring \(\Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr)\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) over the image
567	  of \(V\) is identified with \(\Gamma(U, V)\) by the canonical \emph{ring
568	  isomorphism}
569	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V : \Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr) \xrightarrow{\sim}
570	  \Gamma(U, V)\)
571	  (the \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) isomorphism
572	  \(\mathtt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.appIso}\)). Restriction along \(f\) is
573	  therefore extension of scalars along
574	  this ring isomorphism, and the proof needs no flatness anywhere.
575	
576	  \emph{Step 1 (reduce restriction to the abstract pullback).} The restriction
577	  \((-)|_f\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along the open immersion \(f\) is
578	  naturally isomorphic to the abstract presheaf-of-modules pullback
579	  \((\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(-)\), via the
580	  present comparison \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\). It
581	  therefore suffices to produce the comparison and prove it an isomorphism for
582	  the abstract pullback functor.
583	
584	  \emph{Step 2 (move the pullback inside the sheafification).} The substrate
585	  \(\otimes_X\) is sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor product
586	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}). The pullback commutes with
587	  sheafification: there is a natural isomorphism
588	  \(\mathtt{sheafification} \circ \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
589	   \;\cong\;
590	   \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi \circ \mathtt{sheafification}\)
591	  (the present \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback}\)). Hence the
592	  whole question descends to the presheaf level, where it is the base-change
593	  comparison
594	  \[
595	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(A \otimes B)
596	      \;\longrightarrow\;
597	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,A
598	      \;\otimes\;
599	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,B
600	  \]
601	  for the underlying presheaves of modules \(A, B\) of \(M\) and \(N\).
602	
603	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
604	  % \ref{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback} (the pair $(f^*, f_*)$ of
605	  % pullback/pushforward of $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a morphism of ringed
606	  % spaces is an adjoint pair)
607	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L252-273).
608	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
609	  % "\begin{lemma}
610	  % \label{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback}
611	  % Let $f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$
612	  % be a morphism of ringed spaces.
613	  % \begin{enumerate}
614	  % \item The functor
615	  % $f_* : \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \to \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_Y)$
616	  % is left exact. In fact it commutes with all limits.
617	  % \item The functor
618	  % $f^* : \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_Y) \to \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X)$
619	  % is right exact. In fact it commutes with all colimits.
620	  %  [\dots]
621	  % \end{enumerate}
622	  % \end{lemma}
623	  %  [\dots]
624	  % Parts (1) and (2) hold because $(f^*, f_*)$ is an adjoint pair
625	  % of functors, see
626	  % Sheaves, Lemma \ref{sheaves-lemma-adjoint-pullback-pushforward-modules}"
627	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
628	  \texttt{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback}: pullback and pushforward of
629	  $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a morphism form an adjoint pair $(f^*, f_*)$, the
630	  abstract universal property on which the comparison below rests.}
631	
632	  \emph{Step 3 (the genuine residual: H1 alone).} Completing this comparison at the
633	  presheaf level requires a sectionwise handle on the abstract functor
634	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\), which is defined as the left
635	  adjoint of pushforward (the presheaf-of-modules avatar of the
636	  \(f^* \dashv f_*\) adjunction of the cited Stacks lemma) and admits no
637	  sectionwise formula in Mathlib at the pinned commit. The closure passes through
638	  two presheaf-level ingredients, of which exactly one now remains open.
639	
640	  The first ingredient --- a strong-monoidal upgrade of
641	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along the structure-sheaf ring \emph{isomorphism}
642	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\), so that base change along the isomorphism commutes with
643	  \(\otimes\) --- is \emph{closed in this chapter} as
644	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
645	  (\(\mathtt{restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv}\)): for a ring isomorphism
646	  \(e : R \simeq S\) the canonical map \(a \otimes_R b \mapsto a \otimes_S b\) is an
647	  \(R\)-linear equivalence \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M) \otimes_R
648	  \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(N) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M
649	  \otimes_S N)\).
650	
651	  The \emph{sole remaining residual} is therefore the presheaf-level identification
652	  \[
653	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \;\cong\; \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
654	  \]
655	  via \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\). One exhibits a
656	  presheaf-level adjunction
657	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) and concludes
658	  that \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\), being a left adjoint of
659	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\), is canonically isomorphic to the abstract left
660	  adjoint \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\). Here \(\beta\) is the structure map of the
661	  restriction functor, so that \((M|_f).\mathrm{val} =
662	  (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,M.\mathrm{val}\) definitionally;
663	  composing the resulting comparison with
664	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv} yields the asserted isomorphism.
665	
666	  This presheaf-level adjunction is the presheaf analogue of the sheaf-level
667	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\), and must be built from
668	  presheaf-level \(\mathtt{pushforwardNatTrans}\) and
669	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardCongr}\). These two building blocks are \emph{Mathlib-absent}
670	  at the pinned commit: only the \emph{sheaf}-level versions exist
671	  (\texttt{Mathlib/.../SheafOfModules/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean}), whereas
672	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardId}\) and \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) do already exist at the
673	  presheaf level. This residual is being constructed \emph{directly}, mirroring the
674	  existing sheaf-level template app-for-app at the presheaf level (a Mathlib-gradient
675	  build, not a deferral to an upstream PR).
676	
677	  This lemma is the single substrate linchpin of the relative Picard group law for
678	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(M, N\): the associator
679	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} consumes the comparison
680	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_f \cong M|_f \otimes_U N|_f\) on its sole consumer
681	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}, where the restriction is commuted
682	  past the \emph{sheafified} tensor \(M \otimes_X N\) \emph{before} any local
683	  trivialisation of \(M, N\) is introduced --- so the comparison is required for the
684	  global, untrivialised modules, and no free-cover specialisation can stand in for it.
685	  Consequently the H1 residual \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong
686	  \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) is genuinely on the critical path, and closing it is
687	  the \emph{single open substrate obligation} of this chapter. The superseded
688	  route~(e) \((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal} \to \mathtt{Sheaf.monoidalCategory}\)
689	  (\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}) remains named only as a last-resort fallback should the
690	  presheaf-level adjunction itself bottom out; it is not the plan.
691	\end{proof}
692	
693	\begin{lemma}
694	[Presheaf-level pushforward adjunction and strong-monoidal restriction (H1/H2 substrate)]
695	  \label{lem:presheaf_pushforward_adj_substrate}
696	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans,
697	        PresheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr,
698	        PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj,
699	        PresheafOfModules.isIso_of_isIso_app,
700	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective}
701	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
702	  These five presheaf-level declarations are the two ingredients on which the
703	  linchpin \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} rests; both are absent from Mathlib at
704	  the pinned commit and are supplied project-side.
705	
706	  \emph{H1 (the pushforward adjunction).} For a morphism \(\beta\) of presheaves of
707	  rings, \(\mathtt{pushforwardNatTrans}\) and \(\mathtt{pushforwardCongr}\) are the
708	  presheaf-level de-sheafifications of Mathlib's sheaf-level
709	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans}\) and
710	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr}\)
711	  (\texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean}),
712	  reproduced app-for-app one level down at the presheaf of modules. From them
713	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\) assembles the presheaf-level adjunction
714	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\), the
715	  presheaf analogue of the sheaf-level
716	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\). Pairing this against the
717	  existing \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\) through
718	  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\) yields the canonical isomorphism
719	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) of two left
720	  adjoints of the same pushforward --- exactly the residual identified in Step~3 of
721	  the proof of \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}.
722	
723	  \emph{H2 (strong-monoidal restriction).} \(\mathtt{isIso\_of\_isIso\_app}\) is the
724	  sectionwise isomorphism criterion: a morphism of presheaves of modules is an
725	  isomorphism once each of its section maps is. Built on it,
726	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective}\) equips the presheaf-level
727	  restriction-of-scalars functor
728	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\alpha\) with a \emph{strong}-monoidal
729	  structure whenever the base-change comparison \(\alpha\) is \emph{sectionwise
730	  bijective} --- the presheaf-level lift of the ModuleCat-level H2 core
731	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}, assembled section-by-section via
732	  \(\mathtt{Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal}\). It is to be distinguished from the
733	  module-level, ring-equivalence form
734	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsMonoidalOfRingEquiv}\) already pinned in
735	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}: the present declaration is the
736	  \emph{presheaf}-level, \emph{bijective}-comparison form, the one the substrate lift
737	  actually consumes (where the comparison map
738	  \((\alpha.\mathtt{app}\,X).\mathtt{hom}\) is a bijective \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)
739	  morphism but is not syntactically a \((\,\cdot\,)\!.\mathtt{toRingHom}\)).
740	
741	  Together H1 and H2 are the two Mathlib-absent ingredients the linchpin needed; they
742	  are self-contained presheaf-level results and are natural upstream-PR candidates.
743	\end{lemma}
744	
745	\begin{lemma}
746	
747	\leanok
748	[Base change along a ring isomorphism commutes with $\otimes$]
749	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
750	  \lean{restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv}
751	  Let \(e : R \simeq S\) be an isomorphism of commutative rings, and let \(M, N\) be
752	  \(S\)-modules. Restriction of scalars along \(e\) commutes with the tensor
753	  product: the canonical map
754	  \[
755	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M) \otimes_R \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(N)
756	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
757	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\bigl(M \otimes_S N\bigr),
758	    \qquad a \otimes_R b \;\longmapsto\; a \otimes_S b,
759	  \]
760	  is an \(R\)-linear equivalence. Equivalently, \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\) is
761	  \emph{strong} monoidal, not merely lax, when \(e\) is a ring \emph{isomorphism}:
762	  base change along an isomorphism \(R \simeq S\) identifies the two ground rings, so
763	  the lax structure map of \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\) is invertible. The
764	  forward map is \(R\)-linear, but its inverse \(a \otimes_S b \mapsto a \otimes_R b\)
765	  is only \emph{additive} (it is not \(S\)-linear), so the inverse is constructed as
766	  a homomorphism of additive groups and then verified to be the two-sided inverse of
767	  the forward \(R\)-linear map. This closes the strong-monoidal ingredient of
768	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, leaving the presheaf-level pushforward
769	  adjunction as the sole open residual there.
770	\end{lemma}
771	
772	\begin{lemma}
773	
774	[Strong-monoidal upgrade of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along a ring isomorphism (ModuleCat-level H2 core)]
775	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}

1	# Analogy: how to get the tensor-inverse object `Linv` for a locally-trivial `X.Modules`
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	ts219dual
8	
9	## Iteration
10	219
11	
12	## Question
13	
14	To prove `exists_tensorObj_inverse` (every locally-trivial `L : X.Modules` has a
15	locally-trivial `Linv` with `tensorObj L Linv ≅ 𝒪_X`), we must CONSTRUCT an inverse
16	object. (1) Does Mathlib provide an internal-hom/dual for `PresheafOfModules R` /
17	`SheafOfModules R` landing back in modules? (2) Is there a line-bundle-specific
18	shortcut for the inverse (how does `Module.Invertible`/`CommRing.Pic` get the inverse
19	over a fixed ring, and does its shape port sheaf-locally)? (3) Does Mathlib have
20	object-level descent/gluing for `SheafOfModules` or sheaves valued in a category?
21	(4) Verdict + concrete bounded recipe + cost.
22	
23	## Project artifact(s)
24	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean:1390` — `exists_tensorObj_inverse` (the sorry).
25	- `…:991` — `tensorObj` (sheafification of presheaf tensor; the build the dual should mirror — and CAN'T).
26	- `…:1257` — `tensorObj_restrict_iso` (CLOSED iter-217; downstream local→global bookkeeping).
27	- `informal/exists_tensorObj_inverse.md` — iter-218 blocker report.
28	
29	## Decisions identified
30	
31	### Decision 1: internal-hom / dual object for `PresheafOfModules` / `SheafOfModules`
32	
33	- **Mathlib idiom (fixed ring only)**: over a FIXED ring `R`, the internal hom of
34	  `ModuleCat R` IS built: `instance : MonoidalClosed (ModuleCat.{u} R)` with
35	  `ihom M = linearCoyoneda … (op M)`, i.e. `ihom M N = (M ⟶ N)` as an `R`-module, and
36	  `ihom M 𝟙_ = Module.Dual R M = M →ₗ[R] R`. Cite:
37	  `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Closed.lean:39` (instance), `:52` (`ihom_map_apply`).
38	- **For the VARYING-ring case (what the project needs)**: ABSENT at EVERY level.
39	  - No `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R)`, no `MonoidalClosed (SheafOfModules R)`
40	    (re-confirmed: `loogle PresheafOfModules.internalHom` → 0 results; the
41	    `…/ModuleCat/Presheaf/` directory has `Monoidal.lean` (the tensor) but NO
42	    `Closed`/`InternalHom`/`Dual` file; same for `…/ModuleCat/Sheaf/`).
43	  - There is no presheaf-level dual to sheafify either, so this is **NOT** a
44	    "build-presheaf-then-sheafify" mirror of `tensorObj`. `tensorObj` sheafifies cleanly
45	    because the presheaf tensor `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` is COVARIANT in
46	    the restriction maps. The dual/internal-hom is CONTRAVARIANT in the restriction
47	    (`U ↦ Hom_{R(U)}(M(U),R(U))` is not a presheaf in the covariant direction); the
48	    correct object is the slice/end formula `ℋom(M,N)(U) = (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)`, whose
49	    construction IS the hard part. So the iter-217 H1 precedent ("de-sheafify an existing
50	    sheaf-level decl") does NOT apply — there is no parallel API at any level to mirror.
51	- **Gap**: divergent-and-absent (Mathlib has the fixed-ring idiom but nothing for the
52	  varying structure sheaf).
53	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL**.
54	
55	### Decision 2: line-bundle-specific inverse — port the `Module.Dual` shape sheaf-locally?
56	
57	- **Mathlib idiom (fixed ring)**: the inverse of an invertible `R`-module `M` IS its
58	  linear dual. `Module.Invertible R M : Prop := Function.Bijective (contractLeft R M)`
59	  (`Mathlib/RingTheory/PicardGroup.lean:78`); the contraction
60	  `Module.Dual R M ⊗[R] M ≃ₗ[R] R` is `Module.Invertible.linearEquiv` (`:84`); the dual
61	  is itself invertible: `instance : Module.Invertible R (Dual R M)` (`:169`). So Mathlib's
62	  fixed-ring inverse object is **`Module.Dual R M = M →ₗ[R] R = ihom M 𝟙_`** — i.e. the
63	  SAME internal hom as Decision 1, specialised to target `R`.
64	- **Does the shape port?** Sheaf-locally `L|_{U_i} ≅ 𝒪_{U_i}`, and the dual of the
65	  trivial module is trivial, so each LOCAL inverse is just `𝒪_{U_i}`. But ASSEMBLING a
66	  global `Linv` from the local `𝒪_{U_i}` along the inverse-transpose transitions
67	  `g_{ij}⁻ᵀ` is exactly object-level descent (Decision 3). The only way to get a global
68	  inverse WITHOUT descent is a global dual formula — which is the internal hom
69	  (Decision 1). So Decision 2 is not an independent cheaper route: it collapses into
70	  Decision 1 (global formula) or Decision 3 (local-then-glue). The predicate being purely
71	  existential (`∃ Linv, …`) does NOT help — you must still construct at least one object.
72	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** (same gap as Decision 1; no fixed-ring shortcut
73	  evades the object-construction problem).
74	
75	### Decision 3: object-level descent / gluing for `SheafOfModules`
76	
77	- **Mathlib idiom (NEW, 2025, Riou–Merten)**: there IS now an abstract effective-descent
78	  framework. `CategoryTheory.Pseudofunctor.DescentData F f` is the category of objects
79	  over a cover `{X i ⟶ S}` with cocycle descent data
80	  (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Descent/DescentData.lean:57`); the typeclass
81	  `Pseudofunctor.IsStack F J` says the descent functors are essentially surjective
82	  (`…/Descent/IsStack.lean:49`), and `isEquivalence_toDescentData` (`IsStack.lean:70`)
83	  gives effectivity: descent data ⇒ a global object with prescribed restrictions. This is
84	  precisely the route-(II) primitive in the blocker report — assemble a global object from
85	  a cover + cocycle.
86	- **BUT the connection to modules is ABSENT**: there is NO `IsStack` instance for the
87	  module-sheaf pseudofunctor — `grep IsStack` across Mathlib finds it ONLY inside
88	  `Sites/Descent/*`; nothing for `SheafOfModules` / `QuasiCoherent` / `Scheme.Modules`.
89	  There is no constructed pseudofunctor `LocallyDiscrete (Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ᵖ Cat`,
90	  `U ↦ modules on U`, to feed it. The one module-flavoured descent file,
91	  `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Descent.lean`, is FIXED-ring faithfully-flat
92	  comonadic descent (extension of scalars), and even there effectivity is an explicit
93	  `TODO` — it is not Zariski-local object gluing on `Opens X`.
94	- **Gap**: framework present, instance absent. Building the restriction pseudofunctor +
95	  proving `IsStack` for the small Zariski site is a large multi-file effort (the
96	  `DescentData` structure carries `hom`/`pullHom_hom`/`hom_self`/`hom_comp` cocycle fields;
97	  the pseudofunctor needs coherent restriction-of-restriction isos).
98	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** (principled long-term route; heavier than a
99	  bounded build).
100	
101	## Recommendation
102	
103	All three faces are the SAME missing object and all render **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL**.
104	The fixed-ring shape is fully known (`Module.Dual` = `ihom M 𝟙_`), and porting it needs
105	EITHER a sheaf internal-hom of modules (Decision 1/2) OR connecting `SheafOfModules` to
106	the new `Pseudofunctor.IsStack` framework (Decision 3). Neither is a bounded
107	iter-217-style `mathlib-build`: the iter-217 H1 was bounded only because the sheaf-level
108	pushforward adjunction already existed and was de-sheafified — here the internal hom is
109	absent at presheaf, sheaf, AND general-categorical level, so there is no parallel API to
110	mirror, and the dual is contravariant so it is not even a "sheafify the presheaf" job.
111	This is a genuinely LARGE multi-iter swath (comparable to / larger than the abandoned d.2
112	stalk-⊗ effort). The realistic call is to NOT fund the dual build on Lane TS as a bounded
113	objective: either (a) commit to a multi-iter infrastructure block (recommend Decision 1:
114	build the presheaf/sheaf internal-hom of modules, ~several hundred LOC across iters), or
115	(b) escalate to USER for a strategic re-route that does not need the abstract tensor-inverse
116	object (e.g. the divisor-class `Pic⁰` route noted in project memory). Decision 3 (stack
117	framework) is the most principled and reusable if a heavy build is funded, but is the
118	largest.

811	
812	De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.{pushforwardNatTrans, pushforwardCongr,
813	pushforwardPushforwardAdj}`

816	`.val`/`.val.presheaf` presheaf data, so the de-sheafification is mechanical (drop the `Sheaf`
817	wrapper and the sheaf-only `IsContinuous` `letI`s). These are the **H1** linchpin of
818	`tensorObj_restrict_iso`: from a pair `adj : F ⊣ G` one obtains a presheaf-level adjunction
819	`pushforward φ ⊣ pushforward ψ`, hence — against the existing
820	`PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` and via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq` —
821	the iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ` that moves the abstract presheaf pullback onto the concrete
822	restriction pushforward. Per blueprint `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, Step 3 (the H1 residual). -/

828	section PushforwardNatTrans
829	
830	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD
831	
832	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
833	  {F G : C ⥤ D} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
834	  (φ : S ⟶ G.op ⋙ R)
835	
836	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardNatTrans`.** A natural transformation `α : F ⟶ G` of functors
837	`C ⥤ D` induces a natural transformation between the pushforward functors along `F` and `G`.
838	De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans`
839	(`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:154`), dropping the `.val` wrapper. -/
840	noncomputable def pushforwardNatTrans (α : F ⟶ G) :
841	    pushforward.{u} φ ⟶ pushforward.{u} (φ ≫ Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op α) R) where
842	  app M :=
843	    { app := fun U => (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app U).hom).map (M.map (α.app U.unop).op)
844	      naturality := fun {U V} i => by
845	        ext x
846	        dsimp
847	        change (M.presheaf.map (G.map i.unop).op ≫ M.presheaf.map (α.app V.unop).op) _ =
848	          (M.presheaf.map (α.app U.unop).op ≫ M.presheaf.map (F.map i.unop).op) _
849	        simp only [← CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, α.naturality] }
850	  naturality := fun {M N} f => by
851	    ext U x
852	    exact congr($(f.naturality (α.app U.unop).op) x).symm
853	
854	@[simp] lemma pushforwardNatTrans_app_app_apply (α : F ⟶ G) (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R)
855	    (U : Cᵒᵖ) (x) :
856	    ((pushforwardNatTrans φ α).app M).app U x = M.map (α.app U.unop).op x := rfl
857	
858	end PushforwardNatTrans

860	section PushforwardCongr
861	
862	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD
863	
864	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
865	  {F : C ⥤ D} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
866	
867	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardCongr`.** Pushforwards along equal morphisms of presheaves of
868	rings are isomorphic. De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr`
869	(`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:73`), dropping the `fullyFaithfulForget` preimage (the
870	presheaf-level `pushforward` lands in `PresheafOfModules` directly). -/
871	noncomputable def pushforwardCongr {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ) :
872	    pushforward.{u} φ ≅ pushforward.{u} ψ :=
873	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun M ↦
874	    PresheafOfModules.isoMk
875	      (fun U ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr (R := S.obj U) (S := R.obj _)
876	        (f := (φ.app U).hom) (g := (ψ.app U).hom) (by subst e; rfl)).app _)
877	      (fun _ _ _ ↦ by subst e; rfl)) (fun _ ↦ by subst e; rfl)
878	
879	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ)
880	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R) (U x) :
881	    ((pushforwardCongr e).hom.app M).app U x = x := by subst e; rfl
882	
883	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ)
884	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R) (U x) :
885	    ((pushforwardCongr e).inv.app M).app U x = x := by subst e; rfl
886	
887	end PushforwardCongr

889	section PushforwardAdj
890	
891	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD
892	
893	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
894	  {F : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
895	  (adj : F ⊣ G)
896	  (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
897	  (ψ : R ⟶ G.op ⋙ S)
898	  (H₁ : Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.counit) R = ψ ≫ G.op.whiskerLeft φ)
899	  (H₂ : φ ≫ F.op.whiskerLeft ψ ≫ Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.unit) S = 𝟙 S)
900	
901	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
902	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardPushforwardAdj`.** If `F ⊣ G`, then the presheaf-of-modules
903	pushforwards along `F` and `G` are also adjoint. De-sheafification of
904	`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:226`), dropping the
905	sheaf-only `IsContinuous` `letI`s and the `.val`/`.hom` wrappers. This is the H1 linchpin: applied
906	to the open-immersion adjunction `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base` it gives
907	`pushforward β ⊣ pushforward φ`. -/
908	noncomputable def pushforwardPushforwardAdj : pushforward.{u} φ ⊣ pushforward.{u} ψ where
909	  unit :=
910	    (pushforwardId _).inv ≫ pushforwardNatTrans (𝟙 _) adj.counit ≫
911	      (pushforwardCongr (by simpa using H₁)).hom ≫ (pushforwardComp _ _).inv
912	  counit :=
913	    (pushforwardComp _ _).hom ≫ pushforwardNatTrans _ adj.unit ≫
914	      (pushforwardCongr (by simpa using H₂)).hom ≫ (pushforwardId _).hom
915	  left_triangle_components X := by
916	    ext U x
917	    change (X.presheaf.map (adj.counit.app (F.obj U.unop)).op ≫
918	      X.presheaf.map (F.map (adj.unit.app U.unop)).op) _ = _
919	    dsimp only [id_obj]
920	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.left_triangle_components]
921	    simp
922	  right_triangle_components X := by
923	    ext U x
924	    change (X.presheaf.map (G.map (adj.counit.app U.unop)).op ≫
925	      X.presheaf.map (adj.unit.app (G.obj U.unop)).op) _ = _
926	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.right_triangle_components]
927	    simp
928	
929	end PushforwardAdj

931	section StrongMonoidalRestrictScalars
932	
933	universe v'
934	
935	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C]
936	
937	/-- **A sectionwise-isomorphism morphism of presheaves of modules is an isomorphism.**
938	The inverse is assembled sectionwise via `isoMk` (whose forward naturality is exactly the
939	naturality of the given morphism). -/
940	lemma isIso_of_isIso_app {𝓡 : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} 𝓡}
941	    (g : M ⟶ N) (h : ∀ U, IsIso (g.app U)) : IsIso g := by
942	  haveI := h
943	  have hg : g = (PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun U => asIso (g.app U))
944	      (fun _ _ φ => g.naturality φ)).hom :=
945	    PresheafOfModules.hom_ext (fun _ => rfl)
946	  rw [hg]; infer_instance
947	
948	variable {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
949	
950	/-- **`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α` is strong monoidal when `α` is sectionwise
951	bijective.** The lax tensorator `μ` and unit `ε` (`restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`) are assembled
952	sectionwise from the `ModuleCat`-level ones, which are isomorphisms for a bijective ground-ring
953	map (`restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective`, `restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective`); hence the
954	presheaf `μ`/`ε` are sectionwise isos, hence isos (`isIso_of_isIso_app`), and the lax structure
955	upgrades to strong via `Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal`. This is the **H2** presheaf lift of
956	`tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
957	@[implicit_reducible]
958	noncomputable def restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective
959	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
960	    (hα : ∀ U, Function.Bijective (α.app U).hom) :
961	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).Monoidal := by
962	  haveI hε : IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α)) :=
963	    isIso_of_isIso_app _ (fun U => restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (α.app U).hom (hα U))
964	  haveI hμ : ∀ M₁ M₂, IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α) M₁ M₂) :=
965	    fun M₁ M₂ => isIso_of_isIso_app _
966	      (fun U => restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective (α.app U).hom (hα U) (M₁.obj U) (M₂.obj U))
967	  exact Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal _
968	
969	end StrongMonoidalRestrictScalars
970	
971	end PresheafOfModules

978	
979	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
980	
981	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
982	
983	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
984	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
985	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
986	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
987	
988	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
989	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
990	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
991	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
992	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
993	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
994	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
995	
996	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
997	
998	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
999	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
1000	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
1001	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
1002	
1003	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
1004	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
1005	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1006	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1007	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
1008	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1009	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1010	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
1011	
1012	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
1013	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
1014	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
1015	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
1016	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
1017	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
1018	law is definitional. -/
1019	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1020	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1021	
1022	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
1023	
1024	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
1025	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
1026	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
1027	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
1028	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
1029	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
1030	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
1031	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
1032	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
1033	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
1034	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
1035	
1036	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
1037	
1038	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
1039	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
1040	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
1041	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
1042	
1043	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
1044	
1045	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
1046	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
1047	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
1048	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
1049	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
1050	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
1051	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1052	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
1053	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1054	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
1055	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1056	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
1057	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
1058	
1059	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
1060	sheaf.**
1061	
1062	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
1063	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
1064	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
1065	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
1066	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
1067	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
1068	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1069	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
1070	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1071	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
1072	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
1073	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1074	
1075	/-- **Left unitor for `⊗_X`.** `𝒪_X ⊗_X M ≅ M`. (Blueprint
1076	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, left half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor`.)
1077	Sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor `λ_ M.val`, composed with the
1078	sheafification counit identifying `sheafification M.val` with the (already-sheaf)
1079	`M`. The cheap `mapIso` pattern; uses no abstract pullback. -/
1080	noncomputable def tensorObj_left_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
1081	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) M ≅ M :=
1082	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1083	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val) ≪≫
1084	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
1085	
1086	/-- **Right unitor for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X 𝒪_X ≅ M`. (Blueprint
1087	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, right half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor`.)
1088	Sheafification of the presheaf-level right unitor `ρ_ M.val`, composed with the
1089	sheafification counit. -/
1090	noncomputable def tensorObj_right_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
1091	    tensorObj M (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅ M :=
1092	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1093	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).rightUnitor M.val) ≪≫
1094	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
1095	
1096	/-- **Braiding for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X N ≅ N ⊗_X M`. (Blueprint
1097	`lem:tensorobj_comm_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_braiding`.)
1098	The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category is symmetric; its braiding `β_ M.val
1099	N.val` sheafifies to the asserted isomorphism by the cheap `mapIso` pattern. -/
1100	noncomputable def tensorObj_braiding {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) :
1101	    tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj N M :=
1102	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1103	    (BraidedCategory.braiding
1104	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) M.val N.val)
1105	
1106	/-- **Associator for `⊗_X` on `⊗`-invertible objects.** (Blueprint
1107	`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`.)
1108	For `M, N, P` `⊗`-invertible (hence locally free of rank one, hence sectionwise
1109	flat), there is an isomorphism `(M ⊗_X N) ⊗_X P ≅ M ⊗_X (N ⊗_X P)`. This is the
1110	objectwise existence-of-iso datum the group law
1111	`tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid` consumes (associativity as a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)`).
1112	
1113	iter-212 status (typed `sorry`; **go/no-go bridge CLEARED, a NEW residual
1114	located**). The construction is the blueprint's three-step composite. Writing
1115	`a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)`,
1116	`η = sheafificationAdjunction.unit` (the localizer unit, `toPresheaf`-image
1117	`toSheafify ∈ J.W`), and `α` the presheaf-of-modules associator:
1118	  1. `a(η_{M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val} ▷ P.val)` is iso  (P flat ⇒ right-whiskered `η ∈ J.W`
1119	     by `W_whiskerRight_of_flat`; `a` inverts `J.W` by `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W`),
1120	     giving `(M ⊗ N) ⊗ P = a(a(M.val⊗N.val).val ⊗ P.val) ≅ a((M.val⊗N.val) ⊗ P.val)`;
1121	  2. `a.mapIso α : a((M.val⊗N.val)⊗P.val) ≅ a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val))`;
1122	  3. `a(M.val ◁ η_{N.val ⊗ᵖ P.val})` is iso  (M flat), giving
1123	     `a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val)) ≅ a(M.val ⊗ a(N.val⊗P.val).val) = M ⊗ (N ⊗ P)`.
1124	
1125	**The go/no-go bridge `IsIso (a.map f)` from `J.W ((toPresheaf _).map f)` is now
1126	CLOSED, axiom-clean**, as `PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` (it is
1127	`PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms` — the
1128	sheafification functor IS the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)` —
1129	read at one morphism). The right-whisker conjugate
1130	`PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_flat` is also closed axiom-clean.
1131	
1132	**The genuine residual is now the flatness feeding steps 1 and 3**: steps 1/3
1133	need `J.W (toPresheaf (η ▷ P.val))` / `J.W (toPresheaf (M.val ◁ η))`, which
1134	`W_whiskerLeft/Right_of_flat` supply ONLY from the SECTIONWISE flatness instance
1135	`∀ U : (Opens X)ᵒᵖ, Module.Flat (𝒪_X(U)) (P.val(U))` (resp. M). This is NOT
1136	derivable from `IsInvertible`: the only Mathlib route is single-ring
1137	`Module.Invertible R m → Projective → Flat`, which would require `P.val(U)` to be
1138	an invertible `𝒪_X(U)`-module for EVERY open `U` — false in general (the global
1139	sections functor over a non-affine open is not exact and does not preserve
1140	flatness; invertibility is a LOCAL/affine property). The blueprint's
1141	"invertible ⇒ projective ⇒ flat" conflates global module-invertibility with
1142	sectionwise-over-all-opens flatness. The mathematically-correct fix scopes
1143	injectivity LOCALLY (local-triviality whiskering: on the cover where `P ≅ 𝒪`,

1213	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _

1216	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M

1225	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W

2346	\label{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra}
2347	
2348	The PRIMARY group law (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) needs a
2349	\(\otimes\)-inverse for every line bundle, supplied by
2350	\cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} as the dual
2351	\(L^{-1} = \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)\). The standard route
2352	constructs that dual as a special case of the \emph{sheaf internal hom} of
2353	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules; over a fixed ring the internal hom of \(R\)-modules is the
2354	linear-coyoneda module \(\mathtt{ihom}\,M\,N = (M \to N)\), with dual
2355	\(\mathtt{ihom}\,M\,\mathbf{1} = M^\vee\), but for the \emph{varying} structure sheaf
2356	no such
2357	internal-hom / monoidal-closed structure exists at the
2358	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\), \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\), or general-categorical
2359	level. This section decomposes the construction of the missing object into
2360	individually-formalizable sub-steps, following the slice presentation of the internal
2361	hom; each block names the intended Lean declaration and its dependencies. The
2362	specialisation that closes \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} is recorded in
2363	\cref{rem:dual_discharges_inverse}; an alternative descent-theoretic route is noted in
2364	\cref{rem:dual_via_stack}.
2365	
2366	A structural point governs the whole construction. The naive rule
2367	\(U \mapsto \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(M(U), N(U))\) is \emph{contravariant} in the
2368	restriction maps of \(M\) (a restriction \(M(U) \to M(V)\) for \(V \subseteq U\)
2369	induces precomposition \(\Hom(M(V), N(V)) \to \Hom(M(U), \cdot)\) in the wrong
2370	direction), so it is \emph{not} a presheaf in the covariant sense and cannot be
2371	sheafified like the tensor product \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}. The remedy is
2372	the \emph{slice} formula \(U \mapsto (M|_U \to N|_U)\) --- the module of morphisms of
2373	\emph{restricted} modules over the restricted ring --- whose presheaf functoriality
2374	(restrict a global-over-\(U\) morphism to a global-over-\(V\) morphism, for
2375	\(V \subseteq U\)) \emph{is} covariant. Constructing that slice presheaf, and
2376	proving it satisfies the sheaf condition, is the substance of the build.
2377	
2378	\begin{definition}
2379	[Presheaf internal hom of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules]
2380	  \label{def:presheaf_internal_hom}
2381	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.internalHom}
2382	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
2383	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Modules on Ringed Spaces", \S Internal Hom
2384	  % (\texttt{section-internal-hom}, tag area 01CM)
2385	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L3500--L3524).
2386	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2387	  % "Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space.
2388	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$, $\mathcal{G}$ be $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules.
2389	  % Consider the rule
2390	  % $$
2391	  % U \longmapsto \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X|_U}(\mathcal{F}|_U, \mathcal{G}|_U).
2392	  % $$
2393	  % It follows from the discussion in Sheaves, Section
2394	  % \ref{sheaves-section-glueing-sheaves} that this is a sheaf of
2395	  % abelian groups. In addition, given an element
2396	  % $\varphi \in \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X|_U}(\mathcal{F}|_U, \mathcal{G}|_U)$
2397	  % and a section $f \in \mathcal{O}_X(U)$ then we can define
2398	  % $f\varphi \in \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X|_U}(\mathcal{F}|_U, \mathcal{G}|_U)$
2399	  % by either precomposing with multiplication by $f$ on $\mathcal{F}|_U$
2400	  % or postcomposing with multiplication by $f$ on $\mathcal{G}|_U$ (it gives
2401	  % the same result). Hence we in fact get a sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules.
2402	  % We will denote this sheaf
2403	  % $\SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G})$.
2404	  % There is a canonical ``evaluation'' morphism
2405	  % $$
2406	  % \mathcal{F}
2407	  % \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
2408	  % \SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G})
2409	  % \longrightarrow
2410	  % \mathcal{G}.
2411	  % $$"
2412	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], ``Modules on Ringed Spaces'', \S Internal Hom
2413	  (tag area 01CM).}
2414	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of commutative rings on \(\Opens X\) and let
2415	  \(M, N \in \mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\). The \emph{presheaf internal hom}
2416	  \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\) is the presheaf of \(R\)-modules whose value on an open
2417	  \(U\) is the module of morphisms of restricted modules
2418	  \[
2419	    \mathcal{H}om(M, N)(U)
2420	      \;:=\;
2421	    \mathtt{ModuleCat.of}\;\bigl(R(U)\bigr)\;\bigl(M|_U \longrightarrow N|_U\bigr),
2422	  \]
2423	  the \(R(U)\)-module of \(R|_U\)-linear morphisms \(M|_U \to N|_U\) of presheaves of
2424	  modules on \(U\), formed via the open-immersion restriction \(U \hookrightarrow X\).
2425	  For \(V \subseteq U\) the restriction map of \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\) sends a morphism
2426	  \(M|_U \to N|_U\) to its further restriction \(M|_V \to N|_V\); this is covariant in
2427	  \(V \subseteq U\), so \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\) is a genuine presheaf.
2428	
2429	  The slice formula is forced by contravariance: the pointwise rule
2430	  \(U \mapsto \Hom_{R(U)}(M(U), N(U))\) varies \emph{against} the restriction maps of
2431	  \(M\) and so is not a covariant presheaf, whereas the module of morphisms of
2432	  \emph{restricted} objects \(M|_U \to N|_U\) restricts covariantly. Thus, unlike the
2433	  tensor product \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} --- which is covariant in the
2434	  restriction maps and sheafifies directly --- the internal hom must be built through
2435	  the slice presentation, and there is no presheaf-level dual to sheafify naively.
2436	\end{definition}

2438	\begin{definition}
2439	[Presheaf dual against the structure presheaf]
2440	  \label{def:presheaf_dual}
2441	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.dual}
2442	  \uses{def:presheaf_internal_hom}
2443	  With \(R\) and \(M\) as in \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom}, the
2444	  \emph{presheaf dual} of \(M\) is the internal hom into the unit (structure)
2445	  presheaf,
2446	  \[
2447	    M^\vee \;:=\; \mathcal{H}om(M, R),
2448	  \]
2449	  i.e.\ \(M^\vee(U) = \mathtt{ModuleCat.of}\,(R(U))\,(M|_U \to R|_U)\), the
2450	  \(R(U)\)-module of \(R|_U\)-linear functionals on \(M|_U\). This is the
2451	  presheaf-of-modules analogue of the linear dual
2452	  \(\mathtt{Module.Dual}\,R\,M = (M \to R)\) that serves as the \(\otimes\)-inverse of
2453	  an invertible module over a fixed ring; here the fixed-ring dual is taken
2454	  open-by-open and assembled by the slice presentation of
2455	  \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom}.
2456	\end{definition}

2458	\begin{lemma}
2459	[Evaluation/contraction of the internal hom]
2460	  \label{lem:internal_hom_eval}
2461	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.internalHomEval}
2462	  \uses{def:presheaf_dual, def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
2463	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Modules on Ringed Spaces", \S Internal Hom
2464	  % (\texttt{section-internal-hom}, tag area 01CM)
2465	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L3517--L3524).
2466	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2467	  % "There is a canonical ``evaluation'' morphism
2468	  % $$
2469	  % \mathcal{F}
2470	  % \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
2471	  % \SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G})
2472	  % \longrightarrow
2473	  % \mathcal{G}.
2474	  % $$"
2475	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], ``Modules on Ringed Spaces'', \S Internal Hom
2476	  (tag area 01CM).}
2477	  With \(R\) and \(M\) as above, there is a canonical \emph{evaluation} (contraction)
2478	  morphism of presheaves of \(R\)-modules
2479	  \[
2480	    \mathrm{ev}_M \;\colon\; M \otimes_R M^\vee \;\longrightarrow\; R,
2481	    \qquad s \otimes \phi \longmapsto \phi(s),
2482	  \]
2483	  the counit of the tensor--hom adjunction
2484	  \(\mathcal{H}om(M \otimes_R -, R) \cong \mathcal{H}om(-, M^\vee)\) specialised at
2485	  \(R\). On each open \(U\) it is the open-by-open contraction
2486	  \((M|_U) \otimes_{R|_U} (M|_U \to R|_U) \to R|_U\), \(s \otimes \phi \mapsto
2487	  \phi(s)\), and these are compatible with restriction, so they assemble to a morphism
2488	  of presheaves of modules. The tensor product is the presheaf tensor underlying
2489	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}.
2490	\end{lemma}

2492	\begin{proof}
2493	  \uses{def:presheaf_dual, def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
2494	  On a fixed open \(U\), the value \(M^\vee(U) = (M|_U \to R|_U)\) is the module of
2495	  \(R|_U\)-linear functionals on \(M|_U\), and the evaluation
2496	  \(s \otimes \phi \mapsto \phi(s)\) is the standard contraction
2497	  \((M|_U) \otimes_{R(U)} (M|_U \to R|_U) \to R(U)\) bilinear over \(R(U)\), hence a
2498	  well-defined \(R(U)\)-linear map out of the tensor product. For \(V \subseteq U\)
2499	  the square relating the \(U\)- and \(V\)-contractions commutes because evaluating
2500	  then restricting equals restricting both arguments then evaluating
2501	  (\(\phi(s)|_V = (\phi|_V)(s|_V)\)); hence the open-by-open contractions are natural
2502	  in the restriction maps and define a morphism of presheaves of \(R\)-modules. This
2503	  is the counit of the tensor--hom adjunction of \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom}
2504	  specialised to the unit presheaf.
2505	\end{proof}

2507	\begin{lemma}
2508	[The internal hom is a sheaf; the sheaf-level dual]
2509	  \label{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf}
2510	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.dual}
2511	  \uses{def:presheaf_dual, lem:internal_hom_eval}
2512	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Modules on Ringed Spaces", \S Internal Hom
2513	  % (\texttt{section-internal-hom}, tag area 01CM)
2514	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L3502--L3514).
2515	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2516	  % "Consider the rule
2517	  % $$
2518	  % U \longmapsto \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X|_U}(\mathcal{F}|_U, \mathcal{G}|_U).
2519	  % $$
2520	  % It follows from the discussion in Sheaves, Section
2521	  % \ref{sheaves-section-glueing-sheaves} that this is a sheaf of
2522	  % abelian groups. ... Hence we in fact get a sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules."
2523	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], ``Modules on Ringed Spaces'', \S Internal Hom
2524	  (tag area 01CM).}
2525	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(R = X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\), and let
2526	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) with \(N\) a sheaf of modules. Then the
2527	  presheaf internal hom \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\) of \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom}
2528	  satisfies the sheaf condition: for an open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(U\), a family of
2529	  morphisms \(M|_{U_i} \to N|_{U_i}\) agreeing on the overlaps
2530	  \(U_i \cap U_j\) glues to a unique morphism \(M|_U \to N|_U\), because \(N\) is a
2531	  sheaf and morphisms of sheaves of modules are determined and gluable section-wise.
2532	  Hence \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\) descends to an object of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\).
2533	  Specialising to \(N = \mathcal{O}_X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,R\) gives the
2534	  \emph{sheaf-level dual}
2535	  \[
2536	    \mathtt{dual}\,M \;:=\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(M, \mathcal{O}_X)
2537	    \;\in\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X,
2538	  \]
2539	  and the evaluation \cref{lem:internal_hom_eval} descends to a morphism of sheaves of
2540	  modules \(M \otimes_X \mathtt{dual}\,M \to \mathcal{O}_X\).
2541	\end{lemma}
2542	
2543	\begin{proof}
2544	  \uses{def:presheaf_dual, lem:internal_hom_eval}
2545	  Fix an open \(U\) and an open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(U\). A morphism
2546	  \(M|_U \to N|_U\) is a section of \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\) over \(U\); restricting it
2547	  to the \(U_i\) gives a compatible family. Conversely, given morphisms
2548	  \(\varphi_i : M|_{U_i} \to N|_{U_i}\) with \(\varphi_i|_{U_i \cap U_j} =
2549	  \varphi_j|_{U_i \cap U_j}\), section-wise gluing in the sheaf \(N\) produces, for
2550	  each open \(W \subseteq U\) and section \(s \in M(W)\), a well-defined section of
2551	  \(N(W)\) by gluing the \(\varphi_i(s|_{W \cap U_i})\) (these agree on overlaps by
2552	  hypothesis, and \(N\) is a sheaf so the glued section is unique). The resulting
2553	  assignment is \(R(W)\)-linear and natural in \(W\), hence a morphism
2554	  \(M|_U \to N|_U\) restricting to the \(\varphi_i\); uniqueness is the separatedness
2555	  of \(N\). This is exactly the sheaf condition for \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\), so it is a
2556	  sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Taking \(N = \mathcal{O}_X\) yields
2557	  \(\mathtt{dual}\,M\), and the evaluation morphism of \cref{lem:internal_hom_eval},
2558	  being a morphism of presheaves into the sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X\), is a morphism of
2559	  the corresponding sheaves of modules.
2560	\end{proof}

2578	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(L \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) with
2579	  \(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,L\). Then the sheaf-level dual
2580	  \(\mathtt{dual}\,L\) of \cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf} is again locally trivial of
2581	  rank one, i.e.\ \(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,(\mathtt{dual}\,L)\).

2584	\begin{proof}
2585	  \uses{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}

2587	  trivialisation \(L|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\). The internal hom commutes with
2588	  open-immersion restriction --- the slice formula
2589	  \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom} is defined through \(L|_U\), so
2590	  \((\mathtt{dual}\,L)|_U \cong \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_U}(L|_U, \mathcal{O}_U)\),
2591	  the restriction-compatibility being the dual analogue of
2592	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} for the internal hom. Under the trivialisation,
2593	  \[
2594	    (\mathtt{dual}\,L)|_U
2595	      \;\cong\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_U}(\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{O}_U)
2596	      \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_U,
2597	  \]
2598	  the last identification being evaluation at \(1\), the canonical iso of the dual of
2599	  a free rank-one module with itself. Hence \(\mathtt{dual}\,L\) is locally trivial of
2600	  rank one. (Equivalently, this is item~2 of Stacks
2601	  Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-invertible}, tag 01CR: the dual of an invertible

This is a large multi-iteration build. The first axiom-clean brick I can land is the **module structure on `Hom(M,N)` over a base category with a terminal object** — exactly the `R(U)`-module structure the slice formula `ℋom(M,N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` needs (with the restricted site `Over U`, terminal `U`). Let me prototype the cornerstone: the "multiply by a global scalar" endomorphism `μ : R(⊤) → End N`.

Let me verify key API first.

    (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj (Opposite.op T) ⟶

  (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map (hT.from Y.unop).op

  trivial

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory Limits

namespace PresheafOfModules

universe v u₁ u
variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)

noncomputable def termRingMap (Y : Cᵒᵖ) :
    (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj (Opposite.op T) ⟶
      (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj Y :=
  (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map (hT.from Y.unop).op

lemma termRingMap_naturality {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (g : X ⟶ Y) (f) :
    (RingCat.Hom.hom (R.map g)) ((RingCat.Hom.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)
      = (RingCat.Hom.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f := by
  have : (hT.from X.unop).op ≫ g = (hT.from Y.unop).op := by
    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
    apply hT.hom_ext
  simp only [termRingMap, Functor.comp_map]
  rw [← this]
  simp [-IsTerminal.comp_from]

noncomputable def globalSMul (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
    (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) : N ⟶ N :=
  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk
    (fun Y => ModuleCat.ofHom
      (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y)
        ((RingCat.Hom.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)))
    (by
      intro X Y g
      ext m
      simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_map_hom, ModuleCat.hom_ofHom,
        LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearMap.lsmul_apply]
      rw [PresheafOfModules.map_smul, termRingMap_naturality])

end PresheafOfModules

import Mathlib
open CategoryTheory Limits
namespace PresheafOfModules
universe v u₁ u
variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)
noncomputable abbrev Fr (R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}) := R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat
noncomputable def termRingMap (Y : Cᵒᵖ) : (Fr R).obj (Opposite.op T) ⟶ (Fr R).obj Y :=
  (Fr R).map (hT.from Y.unop).op
lemma termRingMap_naturality {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (g : X ⟶ Y) (f) :
    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((Fr R).map g)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)
      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f := by
  have h : (hT.from X.unop).op ≫ g = (hT.from Y.unop).op := by
    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; apply hT.hom_ext
  unfold termRingMap; rw [← h, Functor.map_comp]; rfl
noncomputable def globalSMul (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (Fr R))
    (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) : N ⟶ N :=
  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk
    (fun Y => ModuleCat.ofHom
      (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)))
    (by
      intro X Y g; ext m
      change (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y)
            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) m)
          = (ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) ((LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj X : Type u) (N.obj X)
            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)) m)
      rw [LinearMap.lsmul_apply, LinearMap.lsmul_apply]; erw [PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
      rw [termRingMap_naturality]; rfl)

lemma globalSMul_app (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (Fr R))
    (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) (Y : Cᵒᵖ) :
    (globalSMul hT N f).app Y =
      ModuleCat.ofHom (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y)
        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)) := rfl

variable {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (Fr R)}

lemma globalSMul_one : globalSMul hT N 1 = 𝟙 N := by
  apply PresheafOfModules.Hom.ext; ext Y m
  rw [globalSMul_app]
  simp [map_one]

lemma globalSMul_zero : globalSMul hT N 0 = 0 := by
  apply PresheafOfModules.Hom.ext; ext Y m
  rw [globalSMul_app]
  simp [map_zero]

lemma globalSMul_add (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
    globalSMul hT N (f + g) = globalSMul hT N f + globalSMul hT N g := by
  apply PresheafOfModules.Hom.ext; ext Y m
  rw [globalSMul_app, globalSMul_app, globalSMul_app]
  simp [map_add, add_smul]

lemma globalSMul_mul (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
    globalSMul hT N (f * g) = globalSMul hT N g ≫ globalSMul hT N f := by
  apply PresheafOfModules.Hom.ext; ext Y m
  rw [globalSMul_app, globalSMul_app, globalSMul_app]
  simp [map_mul, mul_smul, mul_comm]

end PresheafOfModules
